userStories.title: Base canvas setup
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a full-screen visual canvas when I open the site so that the experience immediately feels immersive.
userStories.notes: Use WebGL via Three.js or raw WebGL

userStories.title: Collect ephemeral visitor signals
userStories.description: As a system, I want to collect available browser/device signals so that I can generate a unique visual seed.
acceptanceCriteria: Collect userAgent, language, timezone, screen size
Collect devicePixelRatio and hardwareConcurrency if available
Collect pointer movement as runtime entropy
No data is persisted (no localStorage, cookies, or backend calls)
userStories.notes: All data must remain in-memory only

userStories.title: Optional coarse location input
userStories.description: As a system, I want to include coarse location data if easily available so that visuals gain geographic variation.
acceptanceCriteria: Read country/region from Vercel headers if present
Location is reduced to low-entropy form (e.g., country code)
userStories.notes: Do not use browser geolocation API

userStories.title: Generate deterministic session seed
userStories.description: As a system, I want to transform collected signals into a session seed so that visuals are consistent within a session.
acceptanceCriteria: Hash combined signals into a numeric seed
Seed remains stable during session runtime
userStories.notes: Use lightweight hash (e.g., murmur or custom)

userStories.title: Initial generative visual scene
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a generated visual scene based on my session seed so that the experience feels unique.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one geometry system rendered (points/lines/mesh)
Animation loop runs at >= 30fps on desktop
No user interaction required to start visuals
userStories.notes: Keep first scene minimal (point cloud or particle system)

userStories.title: Audio playback system
userStories.description: As a user, I want background music to play so that the visual experience is audiovisual.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio attempts autoplay in muted state
Audio loops or continues with next random track

userStories.title: Audio analysis pipeline
userStories.description: As a system, I want to analyse audio in real time so that visuals can react to sound.
userStories.notes: Expose analyser data to render loop

userStories.title: Audio-reactive visuals
userStories.description: As a user, I want visuals to respond to audio so that the experience feels alive.
acceptanceCriteria: Visual parameters (scale, color, motion) react to frequency bands
Low/high frequency bands influence different properties
Works even when muted (analysis still runs)
userStories.notes: Map frequency bins to shader uniforms or particle behavior

userStories.title: Mute/unmute control
userStories.description: As a user, I want to toggle sound so that I can choose between silent and audio modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggles audio gain without stopping analyser

userStories.title: Pointer-driven entropy
userStories.description: As a system, I want to incorporate pointer movement so that visuals evolve with user interaction.

userStories.title: Info overlay
userStories.description: As a user, I want to understand what the experience does so that I am aware of the artistic and privacy intent.
acceptanceCriteria: Explicit statement: no data stored or tracked
Overlay can be opened/closed without reloading scene

userStories.title: Time-based evolution
userStories.description: As a user, I want the scene to evolve over time so that the experience feels dynamic.
acceptanceCriteria: Scene changes over runtime without reload
Different sessions produce different temporal behavior

userStories.title: Multiple visual modes
userStories.description: As a system, I want to support multiple visual styles so that variation increases over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Mode selected based on seed or time

userStories.title: Mobile fallback performance
userStories.description: As a user on mobile, I want the experience to run acceptably so that it is still usable.
acceptanceCriteria: Reduced particle count on small screens
